title: Change "Weekend" format on a Chart/Workload Widget Level
excerpt:
Description
I would like to be able to change the Weekends format (Friday-Saturday vs. Saturday-Sunday) on a widget level specifically for charts and the Workload Widget. Right now, the only way I can change Weekends for…

posts.author: beth1021 (Elisabeth Juliano)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I would like to be able to change the Weekends format (Friday-Saturday vs. Saturday-Sunday) on a widget level specifically for charts and the Workload Widget. Right now, the only way I can change Weekends format is on the Account level.

What are you trying to achieve

We have employees around the globe. Half of employees have a work week of Monday through Sunday. The other half have a work week of Sunday through Saturday. It makes it difficult to see accurate project and time data for the correct week in the charts/workload widget.

I have tried to customize Work Schedules for Teams, however it doesn’t solve the issue since a lot of employees can work any day of the week depending on which project they work on and don’t necessarily have a set schedule.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes to this! I can definitely see how that would be a problem for globally distributed teams.

Right now the weekend definition being locked at the account level makes it difficult when different regions operate on different work weeks. Being able to set the weekend configuration at the widget level for things like the Workload and Chart widgets would make a lot of sense so dashboards can reflect the correct working week without affecting the entire account.

I don’t personally have teams working different weekend schedules, but I run into a similar limitation when building dashboards in accounts where I’m not the admin controlling those account settings. In those cases you’re kind of stuck with whatever the global configuration is.

It also trips me up in smaller ways too. Something as simple as the week starting on Monday vs Sunday in the calendar widget has caused me to put things on the wrong days times than I’d like to admit.

Even though my use case is a little different, I completely get why having that flexibility at the widget level would help a lot.
